action.reasoning: Observation: The previous iteration successfully used cleanlab to filter noisy labels and trained a ConvNeXt-Tiny model on the clean subset, achieving a new best accuracy of 0.8119. The iteration took ~70 minutes, leaving only ~49 minutes in the time budget. Analysis: We do not have enough time to run another full cleanlab pipeline or a long training session. However, we can squeeze out extra performance for free by using Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) and Ensembling. The workspace contains both `best.pt` (best standard model) and `best_ema.pt` (best Exponential Moving Average model). Decision: I will rewrite `predict.py` to load both models, apply Horizontal Flip TTA, and average their predictions. This requires zero training time and should provide a solid boost to the final accuracy. I will submit this for evaluation.
